senses.definition: the plural of semifreddo, a dessert that is halfway between ice cream and mousse, typically served softly frozen
examples: We shared two semifreddos after the lunch.
The café offers several flavors of semifreddos.

senses.definition: capable of being semifried; partially fryable or capable of being fried halfway
examples: The batter is semifriable when the oil is not too hot.
Use a semifriable mixture for a lighter coating.

senses.definition: a hypothetical or partial boundary beyond which territory or ideas are not fully explored
examples: The scientists stood at the semifrontier of quantum biology.
Policy makers discussed the semifrontier of space law.

senses.definition: a partial state of confusion or mess; a light muddle
examples: She was in a semifuddle after the move.
The desk was in a semifuddle with papers everywhere.

senses.definition: partially functional; not fully operational
examples: The old printer is semifunctional but still usable for simple tasks.
We replaced the deteriorating component to restore semifunctional status.

senses.definition: a theoretical stance or approach that advocates partial or partial-function theory in a discipline
examples: Semifunctionalism argues for partial applicability of the model.
The seminar contrasted semifunctionalism with functionalism.

senses.definition: in a partially functional manner; to a partial extent in how something functions
examples: The device operates semifunctionally under warm conditions.
Semifunctionally designed software may still run basic tasks.

senses.definition: partially furnished; having some furniture but not complete
examples: The apartment is semifurnished, with a bed and sofa but no appliances.
We rented a semifurnished unit while waiting for the rest of the furniture.

senses.definition: partially fused or united; not completely fused
examples: The joints were semifused after the welding process.
Semifused materials show partial bonding.

senses.definition: the process or result of fusing only partially; partial fusion
examples: Semifusion occurs at lower temperatures during welding.
The material shows semifusion at the grain boundaries.

senses.definition: having an appearance or concept that is almost future-forward but not fully imagined yet
examples: The film used semifuturistic aesthetics with retro-tech vibes.
The design has semifuturistic elements without being overly sci-fi.

senses.definition: to make something semi-official, semi-complete, or partially in a certain state
examples: They decided to semify the agreement with a provisional signature.
The project was semified to meet the deadline.

senses.definition: a term possibly referring to a semi-formal gala event; a gala with a less formal style
examples: The charity held a semigala instead of a full black-tie gala.
Attendees wore semi-formal outfits to the semigala.

senses.definition: partly gelatinous in texture; having a soft, jelly-like quality
examples: The sauce had a semifgelatinous texture.
The dessert was semif gelatinous yet smooth.

senses.definition: a man who is semi-chivalrous or politely courteous; not fully formal but gentlemanly
examples: He was a semifriendly, semigentleman who opened doors for others.
The semifgentleman was well-dressed and kind.

senses.definition: a partial or half-hearted bow or gesture of respect
examples: He gave a semigenuflection to acknowledge the host.
The semigenuflection was polite but not formal.

senses.definition: partially geometric in form or pattern; relating to both geometric and non-geometric aspects
examples: The design uses semigeometric shapes that hint at mathematics.
The artwork combines semigeometric and organic elements.

senses.definition: alternate spelling of semigeometric; partially geometric in form
examples: The board featured semigeometrical grids.
This semigeometrical motif repeats across the page.

senses.definition: in a way that relates to partially geometric form or pattern
examples: The design was semigeometrically balanced.
The layout is semigeometrically structured.

senses.definition: a structural element that partially acts as a girder; a partial support beam
examples: The building used a semigirder in the frame.
Engineers inspected the semigirder for stress signs.

senses.definition: to glaze only partly; to apply a glaze to a surface in a partial manner
examples: They decided to semiglaze the ceramic to keep some of the glaze off the bottom.
The pot was semiglazed for a rustic look.

senses.definition: partially glazed; having a partial glaze finish
examples: The cup was semiglazed, giving a matte and glossy mix.
A semiglazed bowl catches the light nicely.

senses.definition: a partially spherical object; a globe that is not fully round
examples: The model shows a semiglobe with flattened regions.
A semiglobe projection can distort distances less than a full globe.

senses.definition: having a partially globe-like shape; not completely spherical
examples: The asteroid has a semiglobose contour.
A semiglobose shape reduces air resistance.

senses.definition: having a shape that is almost globular but not entirely
examples: The seed has a semiglobular outline.
A semiglobular cloud formation appeared above us.
